title: ledum subulatum
intro: Ledum subulatum, also known as Labrador tea, is a perennial evergreen plant native to the Arctic tundra and subalpine regions of North America. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The plant is rich in volatile oils, particularly ledol and borneol, which are responsible for its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Ledum subulatum can be prepared as a tea by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water, or it can be used in tincture form, allowing for the extraction of its medicinal compounds.
